LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS EPISCOPAL HOMES FOUNDATION, EHF FUND, INC., EPISCOPAL SENIOR COMMUNITIES, EPISCOPAL COMMUNITIES FOUNDATION, JOHN TENANT MEMORIAL HOMES, J.T.M. COMMUNITIES AND LYTTON GARDENS SENIOR COMMUNITIES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH FORTY-FIVE DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. ON ITS FACE, THE LIFE CARE CONTRACT CONTAINS EXPRESS PROVISIONS THAT APPEAR TO PRECLUDE RECOVERY BY PLAINTIFF ON THE BASIC THEORY OF THE CASE, I.E. THAT THE EPISCOPAL DIOCESE AND CHURCH WERE OBLIGATED TO EXERCISE CONTINUING OVERSIGHT OVER PLAINTIFF'S RESIDENCE AND/OR THE FOUNDATION THAT OWNED AND/OR OPERATED THE FACILITY. SPECIFICALLY, PLAINTIFF'S CONTRACT IS ONLY WITH THE FOUNDATION. THE CONTRACT RECITALS DECLARE THAT THE CHURCH AND ITS SUBDIVISIONS DO NOT ACCEPT "ANY FINANCIAL, CONTRACTUAL OR OTHER LEGAL RESPONSIBILITY FOR THE FOUNDATION, ITS CONTRACTS, ACTS OR OTHER DUTIES OR LIABILITIES." FURTHER, PARAGRAPH 11 STATES THAT THE RIGHTS OF PLAINTIFF ARE LIMITED BY THE CONTRACT AND THE CONTRACT EXPRESSLY PRECLUDES MANAGEMENT RIGHTS. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

THE EXISTENCE OF THE RELATIONSHIP IS A QUESTION OF FACT AND PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED THE ELEMENTS DESCRIBED IN RICHELLE V. ROMAN CATHOLIC ARCHBISHOP (2003) 106 CAL. APP. 4TH 257, 272, FN. 6. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. IT IS REPETITIVE AND UNNECESSARY IN LIGHT OF PLAINTIFF'S BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM HAS MATURED AND THERE IS NO NEED FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE 11TH CAUSE OF ACTION, THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY. PLAINTIFF IS A DIRECT BENEFICIARY OF THE LIFE CARE CONTRACT AND IS NOT, AS A MATTER OF LAW, A THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY OF THE EPISCOPAL CANONS AS ALLEGED IN PARAGRAPH 154. THE DEMURRER TO THE 13TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR AN ACCOUNTING IS OVERRULED BASED ON THE COURT'S RULING ON THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT NOTED THIS CASE WOULD BENEFIT FROM SINGLE ASSIGNMENT TO A JUDICIAL OFFICER. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-14-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT EPISCOPAL SENIOR COMMUNITIES' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances:
S. Anne Johnson of Hanson Bridgett (777-3200) for defendant and moving party
Matthew Pavone (209-9610) and Frank S. Moore (292-6091) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Overruled as to the 6th cause of action, breach of fiduciary duty. The existence of the relationship is a question of fact and plaintiff has alleged the elements described in Richelle v. Roman Catholic Archbishop (2003) 106 Cal. App. 4th 257, 272, fn. 6. The demurrer is sustained as to the 7th cause of action without leave to amend. It is repetitive and unnecessary in light of plaintiff's breach of contract cause of action. Moreover, plaintiff's claim has matured and there is no need for declaratory relief. The demurrer is sustained as to the 11th cause of action, third party beneficiary. Plaintiff is a direct beneficiary of the life care contract and is not, as a matter of law, a third party beneficiary of the Episcopal canons as alleged in paragraph 154. The demurrer to the 13th cause of action for an accounting is overruled based on the Court's ruling on the 6th cause of action. The Court noted this case would benefit from single assignment to a judicial officer. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-20-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS EPISCOPAL HOMES FOUNDATION, EHF FUND, INC., EPISCOPAL SENIOR COMMUNITIES, EPISCOPAL COMMUNITIES FOUNDATION, JOHN TENANT MEMORIAL HOMES, J.T.M. COMMUNITIES AND LYTTON GARDENS SENIOR COMMUNITIES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: MATTHEW B. PAVONE, ESQUIRE (415.209.9610) AND FRANK S. MOORE, ESQUIRE (415.292.6091) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JAMES A. NAPOLI, ESQUIRE AND S. ANNE JOHNSON, ESQUIRE (HANSON BRIDGETT, 415.995.5060) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES THE TENTATIVE RULING. The demurrer is sustained WITH FORTY-FIVE DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. On its face, the Life Care Contract contains express provisions that appear to preclude recovery by plaintiff on the basic theory of the case, i.e. that the Episcopal Diocese and Church were obligated to exercise continuing oversight over plaintiff's residence and/or the Foundation that owned and/or operated the facility. Specifically, plaintiff's contract is only with the Foundation. The contract recitals declare that the Church and its subdivisions do not accept "any financial, contractual or other legal responsibility for the Foundation, its contracts, acts or other duties or liabilities." Further, paragraph 11 states that the rights of plaintiff are limited by the contract and the contract expressly precludes management rights. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
